pattern: The original range spans the array's minimum to maximum, so the gaps are exactly the integers in that span that are not present.
hints: The endpoints of the range are guaranteed to be the smallest and largest values you actually have.
Put the values in a set, then walk from the minimum to the maximum.
Collect every integer in that inclusive span that is missing from the set, in increasing order.

edge_inputs.description: one value missing in the middle
full range across allowed value bounds

stress.description: random unique subset of a range, endpoints forced present
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    lo = rng.randint(1, 50)
    hi = rng.randint(lo + 1, 100)
    full = list(range(lo, hi + 1))
    keep = {lo, hi}
    inner = full[1:-1]
    rng.shuffle(inner)
    take = rng.randint(0, len(inner))
    for v in inner[:take]:
        keep.add(v)
    nums = list(keep)
    rng.shuffle(nums)
    return [nums]
stress.note: smallest and largest are always present per the problem
